nodes.text: The metric that determines your Instagram value to brands — and how to improve it.

Engagement rate is the single most important metric in Instagram influencer marketing. It determines your value to brands, your algorithmic reach, and your ability to convert followers into income. A 50K account with 6% engagement is more valuable to most advertisers than a 500K account with 0.5% engagement.
Engagement Rate = (Total Likes + Comments) ÷ Total Followers × 100
Example: 1,200 likes + 80 comments on a 50,000 follower account =
Extended ER = (Likes + Comments + Saves + Shares) ÷ Followers × 100
What's a Good Instagram Engagement Rate?

Why does engagement rate decrease as follower count grows?
Larger accounts accumulate more passive and inactive followers over time, diluting the percentage that actively engages. This is normal and expected — absolute engagement numbers still grow.
Does Instagram show brands my engagement rate?
Not directly, but tools like Modash, Upfluence, and HypeAuditor show brands your historical engagement rates. Brands will always check before making a deal.
Do saves count more than likes on Instagram?
Saves signal to the algorithm that content is valuable enough to return to — they're weighted heavily in distribution. For brand deals, brands care most about likes, comments, and reach data.

Engagement rate is your Instagram currency with brands. Invest in growing it — through quality content, community interaction, and strategic posting — and every other metric follows. Use our calculator to track your rate and benchmark your performance.
